comments.text: Current precise gap: source publication accepts an interface-only Math.add/2, but semantic keying still waits on Recursive/ModuleDefined for the imported provider function, so interface-only provider calls do not reach materialization as external call edges.

Refined model: this is not just an interface-only import bug. Compiler2 currently collapses callable identity and executable authority by assuming every FunctionId can be discharged by DefineFunction + activation facts. The real fix is to record callable/body provenance at declaration time, then let later jobs ask which authority discharges a fnid for the purpose they need: ordinary source-backed functions via DefineFunction, protocol callback slots via protocol dispatch to impl callbacks, and interface-only provider exports via provider-boundary external call edges with no local body obligation.

title: Compiler2 callable provenance: separate FunctionId identity from body authority and carry provider-boundary calls without DefineFunction
